name: Volleyball
description: The sport of volleyball — origins, positions, rules, and Olympic history

questions.q: How many players are on each side in indoor volleyball?

source.notes: Three front-row and three back-row players.

questions.q: In beach volleyball, how many players are on each side?

source.notes: Two-person beach volleyball became Olympic in 1996.

questions.q: What is the maximum number of contacts a team can use before hitting the ball back over the net?

source.notes: Typically pass-set-spike; blocks don't count as a contact.

questions.q: What is it called when a serve lands inside the opponent's court untouched for a point?

source.notes: Scores a point instantly.

questions.q: In which U.S. state was volleyball invented in 1895?

source.notes: Not far from where basketball was invented four years earlier.

questions.q: At which YMCA organization-style facility was volleyball born?

source.notes: Also the original birthplace of basketball (1891) and racquetball (1950).

questions.q: What is the term for a powerful overhead offensive hit in volleyball?

source.notes: The go-to point-scoring attack.

questions.q: Who invented volleyball in 1895?

source.notes: A YMCA physical education director.

questions.q: What was the original name Morgan gave to volleyball?

source.notes: Borrowed the name from badminton.

questions.q: The International Volleyball Federation (FIVB) was founded in what year?

source.notes: Headquartered in Lausanne, Switzerland.

questions.q: In what year did volleyball debut as an Olympic sport?

source.notes: At the Tokyo Summer Games.

questions.q: In what year did beach volleyball make its Olympic debut?

source.original_a: Beach volleyball was added to the Olympic program
source.notes: 1996 Atlanta Games.

questions.q: Which specialized defensive position, introduced in 1998, wears a different-colored jersey?

source.notes: Cannot attack above net height or serve (most leagues).

questions.q: Under modern indoor rally scoring, how many points are needed to win a standard set (with a 2-point margin)?

source.notes: Fifth tiebreak set is to 15 instead.

questions.q: What is the men's official indoor volleyball net height in meters?

source.notes: Women's net is 2.24 m.

questions.q: Who, widely regarded as volleyball's GOAT, won Olympic golds in both indoor and beach volleyball for the USA?

source.notes: Golds in 1984, 1988 (indoor) and 1996 (beach).

questions.q: What is a successful offensive attack that scores called in volleyball jargon?

source.notes: An attack that directly results in a point for the hitter.

questions.q: What defensive move saves a hard-driven spike from touching the floor?

source.notes: Often performed with forearms or an open-hand pancake.

questions.q: The FIVB adopted the current rally-point scoring system for indoor play in what year?

source.original_a: Switched to the rally scoring system
source.notes: Replaced side-out scoring; took effect internationally in 1999-2000.

questions.q: Which American observer suggested renaming 'Mintonette' to 'volleyball' after watching an early match?

source.notes: A YMCA physical education professor.

questions.q: Which country won the first Olympic gold medal in men's indoor volleyball in 1964?

source.notes: Japan won the women's gold the same year.

questions.q: Which Japanese manga and anime series has introduced millions of fans worldwide to high-school volleyball?

source.notes: Follows the Karasuno High boys' volleyball team.
